question: Does correction propagation tool maintain quality at N=799 (20 sessions since S385)?

design.method: Run correction_propagation.py --classify at N=799 vs S385 baseline (N=672, 23 uncorrected, 0 HIGH)
design.fix_applied: Self-declaration guard: 60% FP rate eliminated. Require SUPERSEDED/ARCHIVED/RETRACTED marker or falsified/superseded by L-NNN pattern to confirm falsification.

s385_baseline.note: S385 baseline likely also had FP — estimate 6-7 of 11 falsified were genuine

prediction_vs_actual.status_uncorrected: CLOSE — 26 vs 23 baseline (3 new from corpus growth)
prediction_vs_actual.actual_new_falsification: CONFIRMED — L-556, L-567, L-734, L-746 all post-S385
prediction_vs_actual.unexpected_finding: 60% FP rate in falsification detection. Context-matching (L-NNN near FALSIFIED keyword) inflated count without self-declaration validation. FIXED.

interpretation.primary: Correction propagation tool had 60% false positive rate at N=799 (up from ~20% claimed at S383). Root cause: directional regex matches L-NNN appearing near FALSIFIED in another lesson's text about something else (challenge falsifications, third-party claims). Fix: require positive self-declaration.
interpretation.mechanism: As corpus grows, more lessons mention FALSIFIED/SUPERSEDED in contexts where nearby L-NNN references are innocent (cited as evidence, not as the target). Linear growth in false contexts → quadratic FP growth.
interpretation.impact: 155→26 uncorrected citations (6x reduction). 25→11 falsified lessons. FP-adjusted correction rate 35%→50%.
interpretation.residual: 1 HIGH (L-052 <- L-050). 25 citation-only (safe). All content-dependent: 1 item.

tool_changes: correction_propagation.py: added self-declaration guard (default removal for unconfirmed falsification)
correction_propagation.py: expanded self-declaration regex to catch ARCHIVED markers and superseded/replaced by L-NNN
